title: arg_session: Ensure created void* types have arch.
body:
Root cause: SimCC.arg_session may create a void pointer type for implicit out-params using SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()) without attaching an architecture. This leads to a ValueError when size/alignment are queried (size requires an arch). The fix is to attach the current architecture to the void* type at creation time so size/arch-dependent properties are available.

What I propose:
- Change in SimCC.arg_session (return_in_implicit_outparam(ret_ty) branch): construct the void pointer as SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()).with_arch(self.arch) so it carries arch information from the start.
- This is a small, targeted change that preserves existing behavior for other code paths and eliminates the crash when arch is needed to compute size.

Impact:
- Minimal and backwards-compatible. Only affects the implicit outparam path when ret_ty requires a void* as a placeholder for the return value object.
- No behavioral change for already-arch-initialized pointers or other paths that don’t exercise this code path.

step_plan.summary: Attach architecture to the void* placeholder created in SimCC.arg_session for implicit out-params to prevent arch-dependent crashes while keeping changes minimal.

steps.operation: Patch SimCC.arg_session to attach arch to implicit void* placeholder
steps.reason: Currently the implicit outparam placeholder is created as SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()) without an arch, leading to ValueError when size/arch-dependent properties are accessed. Attaching the current architecture ensures size and alignment calculations work reliably across architectures.
steps.side_effects: Minimal risk; only affects the implicit outparam path. Downstream code that queries size or alignment will now have arch information. No behavioral change for other code paths. May require running unit tests; consider adding a targeted unit test for the implicit path.
